description: GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline that builds Windows/macOS/Linux installers on tag push, publishes GitHub Releases, and wires up the in-app auto-update banner so users get notified of new versions.

userStories.title: GitHub Actions release workflow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a GitHub Actions workflow that builds and publishes release artifacts when I push a version tag.
acceptanceCriteria: New file .github/workflows/release.yml
Triggers on push of tags matching v*.*.* (e.g., v1.0.0, v0.2.0-beta.1)
Runs on a matrix of windows-latest, ubuntu-latest, macos-latest
Steps: checkout, setup Node 22, npm ci, npx tsc --noEmit, npx vitest run, then platform-specific build
On Windows runner: runs npm run build:win, collects release/*.exe artifacts
On Linux runner: runs npm run build:linux, collects release/*.AppImage and release/*.deb artifacts
On macOS runner: runs npm run build:mac, collects release/*.dmg artifacts
After all matrix jobs succeed, a final job creates a GitHub Release with tag name as title and attaches all artifacts
Uses softprops/action-gh-release or similar action to create the release with auto-generated release notes
Code signing env vars (CSC_LINK, CSC_KEY_PASSWORD) are read from GitHub Secrets but builds succeed without them
The workflow uses actions/upload-artifact and actions/download-artifact to pass build outputs between jobs
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The build scripts in package.json already include download:octave before electron-builder. The download:octave step downloads ~200MB of Octave binaries per platform. Use timeout-minutes: 30 for build jobs. The release job should download all artifacts from the build matrix and attach them to the GitHub Release. Use actions/upload-artifact@v4 and actions/download-artifact@v4. For the release creation, use softprops/action-gh-release@v2 with generate_release_notes: true.

userStories.title: GitHub Actions CI workflow for PRs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want automated tests running on every push and PR so regressions are caught before merge.
acceptanceCriteria: New file .github/workflows/ci.yml
Triggers on push to any branch and pull_request to main
Runs on windows-latest (matches the development environment)
Steps: checkout, setup Node 22, npm ci, npx tsc --noEmit, npx vitest run
Fails the PR check if typecheck or tests fail
Does NOT build installers or run download:octave (too slow for PRs)
Skips the 1 pre-existing failing test (publish-html-integration Windows path issue)
Total CI time should be under 5 minutes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The test suite has 800+ passing tests and 1 pre-existing failure (publish-html-integration.test.ts Windows path issue). The CI should not fail on this known issue. Either exclude the test file or accept 1 failure. Use continue-on-error or test filtering.

userStories.title: Verify and fix publish config for GitHub repo
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the electron-builder publish config to match the actual GitHub repository so auto-update can find releases.
acceptanceCriteria: Check the actual GitHub remote URL via git remote -v in the project
Update package.json build.publish to have the correct owner and repo matching the GitHub repository
The current config is owner: matslop, repo: matslop — verify this matches the actual repo, update if it should be owner: Rising-Edge-Systems or similar
Add or update the repository field in package.json to point to the correct GitHub URL
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The git remote shows https://github.com/Rising-Edge-Systems/MatSlop.git — so owner should be Rising-Edge-Systems and repo should be MatSlop. Update build.publish accordingly.

userStories.title: Wire updateBridge into app startup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the auto-update bridge initialized on app startup so it checks for updates automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/main/index.ts, after createWindow() resolves, initialize the auto-update bridge
Use createUpdateBridge() from updateBridge.ts with proper deps: getAppVersion from app.getVersion(), sendStatus forwarding to mainWindow.webContents.send('update:status', status)
Store last-check timestamp in electron-store or a simple JSON file in userData
Call checkIfDue() after a 10-second delay on startup (don't block app launch)
Set up a setInterval to call checkIfDue() every hour (the bridge internally decides if 24h has elapsed)
The bridge is already implemented in updateBridge.ts — just wire it up
Typecheck passes
All tests pass
userStories.notes: updateBridge.ts exports createUpdateBridge(deps) which accepts getAppVersion, sendStatus, setLastCheckMs, getLastCheckMs, getIntervalHours. Use app.getVersion() for version. For persistence, use a simple JSON file in app.getPath('userData')/update-state.json or electron-store. The bridge lazily imports electron-updater so it won't crash in dev mode.

userStories.title: Add update IPC bridge methods in preload
userStories.description: As a developer, I need IPC methods for the renderer to trigger update actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Preload script (src/preload/index.ts) exposes window.matslop.updateCheck() that invokes 'update:check' IPC
Preload script exposes window.matslop.updateDownload() that invokes 'update:download' IPC
Preload script exposes window.matslop.updateInstall() that invokes 'update:install' IPC
Preload script exposes window.matslop.onUpdateStatus(callback) that listens for 'update:status' IPC events from main process
Main process (src/main/index.ts) handles 'update:check' by calling updateBridge.checkNow()
Main process handles 'update:download' by triggering the download (electron-updater's autoUpdater.downloadUpdate())
Main process handles 'update:install' by calling updateBridge.quitAndInstall()
Typecheck passes
All tests pass
userStories.notes: The preload already exposes many IPC methods — follow the same pattern. For onUpdateStatus, use ipcRenderer.on('update:status', callback) and return a cleanup function. The download trigger needs access to the electron-updater autoUpdater instance — the updateBridge may need to expose a downloadUpdate() method.

userStories.title: Update banner UI in renderer
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a non-intrusive banner when a new version is available so I can choose to update.
acceptanceCriteria: Check if src/renderer/editor/UpdateBanner.tsx already exists and update it, or create it if not
Banner component uses window.matslop.onUpdateStatus() to listen for update status changes
When status is 'available': shows 'MatSlop v{version} is available' with 'Download' button
When status is 'downloading': shows progress bar with percentage
When status is 'downloaded': shows 'Update ready — Install & Restart' button
When status is 'idle', 'checking', or 'not-available': banner is hidden
When status is 'error': banner shows error message briefly then hides
Banner has a dismiss/close button (X) that hides it until next status change
Banner appears at the top of the editor panel, above the toolbar
Banner uses app CSS variables (--bg-header, --accent-color, --text-primary, etc.) for theming
Clicking 'Download' calls window.matslop.updateDownload()
Clicking 'Install & Restart' calls window.matslop.updateInstall()
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: There is already an UpdateBanner.tsx component — check its current implementation and update it to use the new IPC methods. The banner should be rendered in App.tsx or EditorPanel.tsx above the editor toolbar. Style it similar to the editor-run-warning banner. Use the UpdateStatus type from updateBridge.ts for type safety.

userStories.title: Add Check for Updates menu item
userStories.description: As a user, I want a 'Check for Updates' menu item in the Help menu.
acceptanceCriteria: Help menu in src/main/appMenu.ts has a 'Check for Updates...' item
Clicking it sends a menu action that triggers updateBridge.checkNow()
If an update is found, the update banner appears in the renderer
If no update is available, a native dialog shows 'You are up to date (v{version})'
If the check fails (network error), a native dialog shows the error message
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The appMenu.ts already has a Help menu. Add the item there. Use dialog.showMessageBox for the 'up to date' confirmation. The checkNow() method returns an UpdateStatus — check status.kind to decide which dialog to show.

userStories.title: Version bump npm scripts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want simple npm scripts to bump the version and trigger a release.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json has script version:patch that runs: npm version patch && git push && git push --tags
package.json has script version:minor that runs: npm version minor && git push && git push --tags
package.json has script version:major that runs: npm version major && git push && git push --tags
Create a brief RELEASING.md documenting the release process: npm run version:patch → wait for CI → release appears on GitHub
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: npm version automatically updates package.json, creates a git commit, and creates a git tag. The scripts just add git push && git push --tags to push everything. The tag push triggers the release workflow from US-C01.
